finding: Publish is gated behind readiness blockers revealed one at a time in a tiny chip; fixing one surfaces the next, so all required work is not visible up front.

evidence.quote: Fix SEO title; Publish greyed out
chip → 'Fix content 1 · Cta Label' … chip → 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL'

persona_rationale: I'll click Publish, get one error, fix it, click again, get a new error, four times over. I'd assume the tool is broken or give up and call support.

suggested_fix.current: Blockers revealed one at a time in a chip
suggested_fix.proposed: A checklist that lists every publish blocker at once with a jump-link to each field, fixed in one pass

finding: A required-to-publish field (CTA destination URL) is not on the canvas; it only exists in the Block actions → Edit settings modal, and the blocker text gives no hint where to find it.

evidence.quote: CTA destination URL field inside Block Settings modal
the destination field is NOT on the canvas; it lives in Block actions → Edit settings modal

persona_rationale: The page told me to fix Destination URL but there was no such field anywhere I could see. I hunted the whole canvas. That's a dead stop.

suggested_fix.current: Required CTA destination URL hidden in block settings modal
suggested_fix.proposed: Surface CTA destination on the canvas next to the CTA label, or make the blocker link open that exact field

finding: After confirming publish, a 'Changes published.' toast appears but the inline confirm card stays open asking to publish again, making it look like it failed.

evidence.quote: Confirm publish card still showing after success
'Changes published.' toast appears BUT the confirm card remains open asking to publish again

persona_rationale: I clicked Confirm, it said published, but the same 'Publish this page?' box was still sitting there. I'd click it again thinking the first failed.

suggested_fix.current: Confirm card stays open after a successful publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Close the card on success and show 'Published — view live page [link]'

finding: No 'Open live page' link appears after publishing in the default panel; the public URL had to be typed by hand.

evidence.quote: No 'Open live page' link was visible after publishing in the default panel state — the live URL had to be typed manually

persona_rationale: I just published. Obviously I want to look at it. Making me copy a URL out of a menu or type it is silly.

suggested_fix.current: No post-publish link to the live page
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a 'View live page' button linking to the public URL the moment it goes live

finding: Three wizard steps stand between 'Create page' and an editable canvas for what is just a blank page.

evidence.quote: Step 1 of 3 — What kind of page are you creating?
3-step choice gate (Resource → Blank page → Start building)

persona_rationale: I clicked Create page to make a page, not to answer a quiz. Three Continues before I can type is three too many.

suggested_fix.current: Three-step type/start wizard before the editor
suggested_fix.proposed: Single screen: pick a type and land in the editor, or default to Resource and go straight in with a type switcher inside

finding: The editor opens with a forced 'Quick Tour · Step 1 of 3' overlay covering the canvas.

evidence.quote: QUICK TOUR · STEP 1 OF 3 / Page structure / This is where the structure of the page is.

persona_rationale: A forced tutorial is exactly the thing I skip. Don't make me dismiss three popups before I can work.

suggested_fix.current: Auto-popping three-step quick tour on first editor load
suggested_fix.proposed: No auto-tour; provide a small '?' / 'Take a tour' button I can ignore

finding: After setting a schedule and saving, the date field re-renders empty and no 'Scheduled for…' status appears anywhere, so there's no way to tell whether it worked.

evidence.quote: the field re-rendered EMPTY and no 'Scheduled for …' status appeared anywhere in the editor
Scheduled publish field

persona_rationale: If I schedule a page to go live Monday and the field shows blank, I have no idea if it's set. I'd re-do it or assume it failed. That's a launch I can't trust.

suggested_fix.current: Schedule field blanks after save with no confirmation
suggested_fix.proposed: After save, show a clear 'Scheduled to publish {date} {time}' banner near the publish button

finding: Scheduled-publish help text says 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' for an Australian business.

evidence.quote: Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles). Leave blank unless this page should publish later.
Label says 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' — for an Australian business

persona_rationale: I'd type 9am thinking my time and the page would go live in the middle of my night. A real mistake waiting to happen.

suggested_fix.proposed: Use the business's own timezone (Australia) or show local time with the offset

finding: The scheduled-publish control is buried at the bottom of the SEO panel inside 'Advanced SEO', far from the Publish button.

evidence.quote: Scheduled publish field is buried at the bottom of SEO panel → Advanced SEO governance area
Scheduled publish under Social description in Advanced SEO

persona_rationale: Scheduling a publish is a publishing decision, not an 'advanced SEO' setting. I'd never think to look under there.

suggested_fix.proposed: Put 'Publish now / Schedule for later' together as one choice at the publish control

finding: The redirects table has no edit or delete control, so a typo'd redirect is permanent from this screen.

evidence.quote: Redirects table has no delete/edit controls — created redirects cannot be removed from this UI.

persona_rationale: If I send an old URL to the wrong place, I can't fix it here. A bad redirect can break traffic and I'd be stuck.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add Edit and Delete per row, with a confirm on delete

finding: No revision history or page-level undo exists until after the first publish, so wrecking a draft before publishing cannot be rolled back.

evidence.quote: revisions do NOT exist while drafting … 'Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.'

persona_rationale: I'd assume 'Save draft' gives me a history to fall back on. If I mangle a draft and there's no undo, that's lost work I can't get back.

suggested_fix.current: Restore points only exist after first publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Snapshot on each draft save so there's always something to roll back to

finding: Typing a page title then navigating away discards it silently with no unsaved-changes warning.

evidence.quote: navigated straight to /admin/pages with NO unsaved-changes warning — typed title silently lost
leave unsaved editor

persona_rationale: I get interrupted constantly. If clicking away throws out what I typed with no warning, I lose work without even knowing.

suggested_fix.current: Silent data loss when leaving an unsaved new page
suggested_fix.proposed: Prompt 'You have unsaved changes — leave anyway?' before discarding

finding: The blocker chip and the readiness panel show two different messages for the same gate at the same time ('Fix SEO title' vs 'Add a hero headline before publishing').

evidence.quote: Chip said 'Fix SEO title' while readiness panel said 'Add a hero headline before publishing.' — fixed the wrong thing first

persona_rationale: One spot tells me to fix the title, another tells me to fix the headline. I fixed the wrong thing first and wasted clicks. Just tell me one thing.

suggested_fix.current: Chip and panel show conflicting blocker messages
suggested_fix.proposed: Use one consistent blocker label in both the chip and the panel

finding: The right-hand SEO/readiness panel scrolls through seven stacked sections (Readiness, Action Items, Search Preview, Builder support, Revision history, Draft preview, Governance comments), heavy chrome around a simple page.

evidence.quote: Long stacked SEO panel: Readiness, Action Items, Search Preview, Builder support, Revision history, Draft preview, Governance comments

persona_rationale: I put a headline and an FAQ on a page. I don't need 'Governance comments' and an 'Approved claims and CTAs' library staring at me. It's clutter around the two things I actually do.

suggested_fix.current: All SEO/governance sections always open in one long panel
suggested_fix.proposed: Collapse advanced sections (Governance comments, Builder support) by default; keep publish + readiness on top

finding: Duplicating a page produces a junk auto-slug ('draft-4020bd0f') instead of one derived from the title.

evidence.quote: created 'Copy of {title}' with auto slug 'draft-4020bd0f' (original slug not preserved/derived)

persona_rationale: A URL like 'draft-4020bd0f' is meaningless and I'd have to fix it by hand every time I copy a page.

suggested_fix.current: Duplicate gets a random slug like 'draft-4020bd0f'
suggested_fix.proposed: Derive the duplicate slug from the title, e.g. 'copy-of-{slug}'

finding: Archive and other row actions are one-row-at-a-time only; there is no bulk select to tidy several pages at once.

evidence.quote: Archiving is per-row only; no bulk select

persona_rationale: When I'm cleaning up old test pages, doing them one menu at a time is tedious. Not a blocker, just annoying.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add checkboxes and a bulk archive action
